articles.title: Metro Cebu air quality turns unhealthy anew amid heat, lack of rain
articles.description: TAGBILARAN CITY, Bohol — Air quality in Metro Cebu was classified as unhealthy anew for vulnerable groups, the Environmental Management Bureau (EMB) – Region 7 reported on Saturday. In its latest monitoring, EMB-7 said air quality has been fluctuating between “fair” and “unhealthy for sensitive groups” over the past five days, with a noticeable increase in fine particulate matter (PM2.5). PM2.5 refers to extremely fine particles in the air measuring 2.5 micrometers or smaller—about 30 times thinner than a human hair. The monitoring station in Talisay City recorded AQI levels considered unsafe for individuals with health risks. The agency said [...]... Keep on reading: Metro Cebu air quality turns unhealthy anew amid heat, lack of rain

articles.title: ICE planning facility for children and families on Pfas-contaminated site
articles.description: Toxic ‘forever chemicals’ found at least 41m ppt in groundwater at former military facility in Louisiana Donald Trump’s Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) is planning a detention facility for children and their families on one the nation’s most Pfas-contaminated sites, which also serves as a hub for the president’s deportation program. The England air force base, now called England Airpark, is a sprawling former military facility in Louisiana where Pfas levels in the groundwater have been found at least 41m parts per trillion (ppt). articles.title: What is the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation, and why are scientists worried about it slowing down?
articles.description: The Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC) helps nourish aquatic ecosystems and is part of the reason Europe has milder temperatures than places in Canada at the same latitude. But lately scientists are concerned that it is slowing down and could even potentially collapse.

articles.title: Rare Rafflesia flower thriving in Bulacan’s biodiversity haven
articles.description: DOÑA REMEDIOS TRINIDAD, Bulacan — The rare Rafflesia lagascae, known for its reddish bloom and pungent odor, continues to thrive in the forests of this town, officials said on Saturday. Mayor Rolando Flores said more sightings are likely in mountainous barangays such as Sapang Bulac and Kabayunan, noting that the town’s forests remain well-preserved and protected from illegal activities. The flower was recently rediscovered in Barangay Camachin by the Department of Environment and Natural Resources–Community Environment and Natural Resources Office (CENRO) Baliwag and the town’s environment office, two years after its first documented sighting in Barangay Kalawakan in 2022. The [...]... Keep on reading: Rare Rafflesia flower thriving in Bulacan’s biodiversity haven

articles.title: Overnight attack in Dnipro injures at least 14, emergency services say
articles.description: At least 14 people, including one child, have been injured as a result of an overnight attack in Ukraine's Dnipro, State Emergency Service of Ukraine said Saturday.

articles.title: Butuan City bracing for scorching weekend, water supply woes
articles.description: SAN FRANCISCO, Agusan del Sur – As extreme temperatures continue to affect large portions of the country, Butuan City residents are preparing for scorching weekend, with the state weather bureau forecasting a heat index that could reach 45°C on Saturday. According to a social media advisory of Butuan City Public Information Office (PIO), this places Butuan under the “extreme caution” heat index category. Across the country, heat indices are expected to range from 42°C to 51°C, increasing the risk of heat-related illnesses. The PIO emphasized the health risks associated with these temperatures, including heat cramps, heat exhaustion, and heat stroke, [...]... Keep on reading: Butuan City bracing for scorching weekend, water supply woes

articles.title: Cebu heat index may reach 40°C on Saturday
articles.description: LAPU-LAPU CITY, Cebu — Generally fair weather will continue in Cebu on Saturday, April 25, but hot and humid conditions may push the heat index up to 40 degrees Celsius, the state weather bureau said. In its daily forecast, the Visayas Pagasa Regional Services Division reported that actual temperatures in the province may range from 26 to 32 degrees Celsius. The heat index, however, may climb as high as 40 degrees Celsius, which falls under the “extreme caution” category. READ: Cebu heat index reaches 37°C on Friday; rain likely by late weekend The bureau explained that the heat index measures [...]... Keep on reading: Cebu heat index may reach 40°C on Saturday

articles.title: Violent tornado tears through Oklahoma town, destroys homes
articles.description: ENID, Okla. (AP) — Raeann Hunt scrambled to her cellar as a tornado bore down on her Oklahoma community. “It is headed right for us,” she recalled thinking, as she peeked outside, unable to contain her curiosity. Huddled inside the dark 8-by-8 foot (2.44-by-2.44 meters) concrete shelter with her husband, brother-in-law, son and a neighbor,

articles.title: Everything but the kitchen sink: how to choose more sustainable and durable cookware
articles.description: What to donate, buy and borrow to free up space, use fewer resources and give your kitchenware a great shelf life Change by degrees offers life hacks and sustainable living tips each Saturday to help reduce your household’s carbon footprint Got a question or tip for reducing household emissions? Email us at changebydegrees@theguardian.com As a baby boomer and grandmother, I’m keen to find ways in my kitchen to try to make up for the damage wrought by my generation and help keep the planet sustainable for my children and grandchildren. As a former professional cook, I have the culinary tools to feed my growing family but am only now acquiring the knowledge to do so in a sustainable way by making small changes at minimal or no cost.
